treatments.name: BPC-157
treatments.description: A pentadecapeptide derived from human gastric juice with powerful tissue-healing, anti-inflammatory, and gut-protective properties. One of the most researched and community-validated peptides in the longevity space.

outcomes.name: Gut Healing & Repair
outcomes.description: Strong evidence for treating leaky gut, IBD, and ulcerative conditions. Multiple animal models and emerging human data showing tissue regeneration.

outcomes.name: Tendon & Ligament Repair
outcomes.description: Robust evidence for accelerating connective tissue healing. Widely used post-surgery (ACL, rotator cuff) with consistent positive outcomes.

outcomes.name: Pain Reduction
outcomes.description: Unexpected but widely reported analgesic effects. Community members report relief from chronic neck, back, and joint pain within days.

outcomes.name: Neuroprotection
outcomes.description: Emerging evidence for brain injury recovery and neuroprotective effects. Promising but primarily from animal studies. Human data limited.

quotes.text: Resolved 2-year chronic neck pain within days of starting BPC Gold orally
Gut issues from 15 years of NSAIDs dramatically improved in 3 weeks

protocols.label: Oral (Gut Healing)
protocols.notes: BPC Gold tablets or equivalent. Take on empty stomach.

protocols.label: Injectable (Tissue Repair)
protocols.notes: SubQ injection near injury site. 4-8 week cycles.

protocols.label: Recovery Stack
protocols.notes: The "Wolverine Protocol" for post-surgical recovery.

keyStudies.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: Novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract
keyStudies.finding: Comprehensive review demonstrating BPC-157 therapeutic potential across multiple GI conditions including IBD, fistulas, and short bowel syndrome.

keyStudies.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 enhances the growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts
keyStudies.finding: BPC-157 promotes tendon healing by upregulating growth hormone receptor expression, accelerating collagen formation in injured tendons.

keyStudies.title: Brain-gut axis and pentadecapeptide BPC 157: Theoretical and practical implications
keyStudies.finding: BPC-157 demonstrates neuroprotective effects via the brain-gut axis, with evidence for dopaminergic system modulation.

treatments.name: GHK-Cu
treatments.description: A naturally occurring copper peptide with remarkable anti-aging, hair growth, and skin rejuvenation properties. Known for reversing grey hair and improving skin elasticity.

outcomes.name: Hair Regrowth & Darkening
outcomes.description: Widely reported hair darkening within 1-2 weeks. Community data shows significant hair regrowth with sustained use.

outcomes.name: Skin Rejuvenation
outcomes.description: Strong evidence for collagen synthesis, wound healing, and skin firmness. Used in cosmetic dermatology for decades.

outcomes.name: Tissue Remodeling
outcomes.description: Promotes tissue repair and remodeling through collagen and decorin upregulation. Emerging data on broader anti-aging effects.

quotes.text: Hair started darkening noticeably within 10 days — wife noticed before I did
Skin texture improved dramatically. Friends asking what I changed in my routine

protocols.label: Topical (Skin/Hair)
protocols.notes: Apply to scalp or face. Best results with microneedling.

protocols.label: Injectable (Systemic)
protocols.notes: SubQ injection. 4-8 week cycles for anti-aging.

protocols.label: Oral
protocols.notes: Less studied route but growing community support.

keyStudies.title: GHK peptide as a natural modulator of multiple cellular pathways in skin regeneration
keyStudies.finding: GHK-Cu activates over 4,000 genes involved in tissue remodeling, promoting collagen synthesis, anti-inflammatory responses, and DNA repair.

keyStudies.title: The human tripeptide GHK-Cu in prevention of oxidative stress and degenerative conditions of aging
keyStudies.finding: GHK-Cu suppresses fibrinogen synthesis, acts as a potent anti-oxidant, and reverses gene expression patterns associated with aggressive cancers.

keyStudies.title: Regenerative and protective actions of the GHK-Cu peptide in the light of the new gene data
keyStudies.finding: GHK-Cu resets gene activity to a healthier state, upregulating genes associated with stem cell proliferation and tissue repair.

treatments.name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin
treatments.description: A synergistic peptide combination that stimulates natural growth hormone release. Widely used for anti-aging, body composition improvement, and recovery enhancement.

outcomes.name: Growth Hormone Release
outcomes.description: Well-established GH secretagogue effect. Increases natural GH pulse without shutting down endogenous production.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Improved fat loss and lean mass gains when combined with exercise. Effects are moderate but consistent.

outcomes.name: Sleep Quality
outcomes.description: Many users report deeper sleep and more vivid dreams. GH release primarily occurs during sleep.

quotes.text: Sleep quality improved dramatically within the first week. Feeling more recovered.
Noticeable body composition changes after 6 weeks — leaner and more energy.

protocols.label: Standard Protocol
protocols.notes: SubQ injection before bed. 5 days on, 2 days off.

protocols.label: Aggressive Protocol
protocols.notes: Twice daily (morning + before bed). 12-week cycles.

protocols.label: Maintenance
protocols.notes: 3x per week. For long-term anti-aging maintenance.

keyStudies.title: Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone secretion after a single administration of CJC-1295
keyStudies.finding: Single dose of CJC-1295 produces sustained GH elevation for 6+ days with dose-dependent increases in IGF-1.

keyStudies.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
keyStudies.finding: Ipamorelin selectively releases GH without affecting cortisol or prolactin levels, providing a cleaner hormonal profile than other GH secretagogues.

keyStudies.title: Growth hormone secretagogues: History, mechanism of action, and clinical development
keyStudies.finding: GH secretagogues like ipamorelin show promise for treating age-related GH decline with favorable safety profiles in clinical trials.

treatments.name: Tesamorelin
treatments.description: An FDA-approved GHRH analog primarily used for reducing visceral fat. One of the few peptides with robust clinical trial data in humans for body composition improvement.

outcomes.name: Visceral Fat Reduction
outcomes.description: FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Strong human data showing significant visceral fat reduction.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Reduces trunk fat while preserving lean mass. Effects are dose-dependent.

quotes.text: Visceral fat noticeably reduced after 8 weeks — DEXA scan confirmed.

protocols.label: Standard
protocols.notes: SubQ abdominal injection. FDA-approved dose.

protocols.label: Anti-Aging
protocols.notes: Lower dose for general body composition improvement.

keyStudies.title: Tesamorelin reduces visceral fat and improves body composition in HIV-infected patients
keyStudies.finding: Tesamorelin achieved 15.2% reduction in visceral adipose tissue vs placebo, with improvements in triglycerides and body image.

keyStudies.title: Tesamorelin, a GHRH analog, reduces liver fat and fibrosis in HIV-associated NAFLD
keyStudies.finding: Tesamorelin significantly reduced hepatic fat fraction and prevented liver fibrosis progression in a randomized controlled trial.

keyStudies.title: Effects of tesamorelin on body composition, visceral fat and metabolic parameters
keyStudies.finding: FDA-approved tesamorelin demonstrated consistent visceral fat reduction across multiple phase III trials with minimal adverse effects.

treatments.name: TB-500
treatments.description: A naturally occurring peptide involved in tissue repair, wound healing, and recovery. Often stacked with BPC-157 for the 'Wolverine Protocol' — a popular post-surgery recovery combination.

outcomes.name: Tissue Repair
outcomes.description: Strong preclinical evidence for accelerating tissue healing across multiple tissue types.

outcomes.name: Wound Healing
outcomes.description: Promotes angiogenesis and cell migration to injury sites. Human data limited but animal data robust.

outcomes.name: Hair Growth
outcomes.description: Some reports of hair regrowth as a secondary effect. Mechanism unclear.

quotes.text: Combined with BPC-157 post ACL surgery — recovery was remarkably fast.

protocols.label: Loading Phase
protocols.notes: First 4-6 weeks. SubQ injection.

protocols.label: Maintenance
protocols.notes: After loading. Continue until recovery complete.

protocols.label: Wolverine Stack
protocols.notes: Combined protocol for post-surgical recovery.

keyStudies.title: Thymosin beta 4 promotes angiogenesis, wound healing, and hair growth
keyStudies.finding: TB-500 (thymosin beta-4) accelerates wound healing through promotion of cell migration, angiogenesis, and reduction of inflammation.

keyStudies.title: Thymosin beta-4 activates integrin-linked kinase and promotes cardiac cell migration, survival and cardiac repair
keyStudies.finding: TB-500 protects cardiac cells after ischemic injury and promotes repair through ILK activation and Akt survival pathways.

keyStudies.title: Thymosin beta 4: A multi-functional regenerative peptide
keyStudies.finding: TB-500 demonstrates regenerative capacity across corneal, cardiac, and dermal tissue models with excellent safety profiles.

treatments.name: KPV
treatments.description: A tripeptide derived from alpha-MSH with potent anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties. Gaining attention for gut inflammation and autoimmune conditions.

outcomes.name: Gut Inflammation
outcomes.description: Promising data for IBD and colitis. Anti-inflammatory mechanism well-characterized.

outcomes.name: Immune Modulation
outcomes.description: Downregulates inflammatory cytokines without immunosuppression.

quotes.text: Noticeable improvement in IBS symptoms within 10 days.

protocols.label: Oral (Gut)
protocols.notes: Capsule form. Best on empty stomach.

protocols.label: Injectable
protocols.notes: SubQ for systemic anti-inflammatory effects.

keyStudies.title: Anti-inflammatory effects of alpha-MSH-derived peptide KPV
keyStudies.finding: KPV tripeptide retains full anti-inflammatory activity of alpha-MSH, inhibiting NF-kB activation and inflammatory cytokine production.

keyStudies.title: KPV nanoparticles effectively reduce intestinal inflammation in murine colitis models
keyStudies.finding: Orally administered KPV nanoparticles significantly reduced colitis severity, demonstrating targeted anti-inflammatory action in the gut.

keyStudies.title: Alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone and related tripeptides: Biochemistry and anti-inflammatory potential
keyStudies.finding: Alpha-MSH peptides including KPV show potent anti-inflammatory properties via melanocortin receptors with therapeutic potential for IBD.

treatments.name: MOTS-c
treatments.fullName: Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the 12S rRNA-c
treatments.description: A mitochondrial-derived peptide that enhances metabolic function, improves insulin sensitivity, and promotes exercise-like benefits at the cellular level.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Enhancement
outcomes.description: Activates AMPK pathway, improves glucose metabolism. Animal data strong, human data emerging.

outcomes.name: Exercise Mimetic
outcomes.description: Mimics some benefits of exercise at the cellular level. Early-stage research.

quotes.text: Energy levels noticeably improved. Feels like a metabolic boost.

protocols.label: Standard
protocols.notes: SubQ injection. 8-12 week cycles.

keyStudies.title: MOTS-c is an exercise-induced mitochondrial-encoded regulator of age-dependent physical decline and muscle homeostasis
keyStudies.finding: MOTS-c treatment in aged mice improved physical performance comparable to exercise, regulating skeletal muscle gene expression.

keyStudies.title: The mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c promotes metabolic homeostasis and reduces obesity and insulin resistance
keyStudies.finding: MOTS-c regulates metabolic homeostasis by targeting the methionine-folate cycle, preventing obesity and insulin resistance in mice.

keyStudies.title: Mitochondrial-derived peptides in aging and age-related diseases
keyStudies.finding: MOTS-c represents a new class of mitochondrial signaling peptides that regulate metabolism and may slow age-related metabolic decline.

treatments.name: Selank
treatments.description: A Russian-developed anxiolytic peptide derived from tuftsin. Used for anxiety, cognitive enhancement, and nervous system regulation without sedation or addiction potential.

outcomes.name: Anxiety Reduction
outcomes.description: Approved in Russia as an anxiolytic. Modulates GABA system without benzodiazepine side effects.

outcomes.name: Cognitive Enhancement
outcomes.description: Some evidence for memory and learning improvement. BDNF upregulation observed.

quotes.text: Noticeable anxiety reduction without any sedation. Clean and subtle.

protocols.label: Nasal Spray
protocols.notes: Intranasal. Effects felt within 30 minutes.

protocols.label: Injectable
protocols.notes: SubQ. Longer-lasting effects.

keyStudies.title: Selank modulates the expression of genes involved in inflammation and immune response
keyStudies.finding: Selank significantly modulates expression of 36 genes related to immune function and inflammatory signaling pathways.

keyStudies.title: Anxiolytic effect of the synthetic peptide selank
keyStudies.finding: Selank demonstrates potent anxiolytic activity comparable to benzodiazepines without sedation, addiction potential, or cognitive impairment.

keyStudies.title: Molecular mechanisms of action of selank on the GABAergic system
keyStudies.finding: Selank enhances GABAergic neurotransmission through allosteric modulation, explaining its anti-anxiety and nootropic effects.

treatments.name: Semax
treatments.description: A synthetic peptide analog of ACTH(4-7) developed in Russia for cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and stroke recovery. Promotes BDNF and NGF expression.

outcomes.name: Cognitive Enhancement
outcomes.description: Enhances BDNF expression and cognitive performance. Approved in Russia for cerebrovascular disorders.

outcomes.name: Neuroprotection
outcomes.description: Used in stroke recovery protocols in Russia. Limited Western clinical data.

quotes.text: Improved focus and verbal fluency. Subtle but noticeable.

protocols.label: Nasal Spray
protocols.notes: Intranasal administration. Start low, titrate up.

keyStudies.title: Semax (ACTH 4-10 analog) prevents oxidative damage of brain proteins and lipids following ischemia
keyStudies.finding: Semax provides significant neuroprotection against ischemic brain injury by reducing oxidative stress markers and preserving brain tissue.

keyStudies.title: Semax, an ACTH(4-10) analogue with nootropic properties, activates dopaminergic and serotoninergic brain systems
keyStudies.finding: Semax activates dopamine and serotonin pathways, enhancing attention, memory formation, and learning in preclinical models.

keyStudies.title: Neuroprotective and nootropic drug semax inhibits the expression of proinflammatory cytokines after focal cerebral ischemia
keyStudies.finding: Semax suppresses pro-inflammatory gene expression after stroke, reducing brain inflammation and improving neurological recovery.

treatments.name: Epitalon
treatments.description: A tetrapeptide that activates telomerase, the enzyme responsible for maintaining telomere length. One of the most intriguing anti-aging peptides with decades of Russian research.

outcomes.name: Telomere Maintenance
outcomes.description: Activates telomerase in human cell cultures. Animal studies show lifespan extension. Human clinical data limited.

outcomes.name: Melatonin Regulation
outcomes.description: Normalizes melatonin production in older adults. May improve sleep-wake cycles.

quotes.text: Sleep improved noticeably. Hard to measure anti-aging effects short-term.

protocols.label: Standard Cycle
protocols.notes: SubQ injection for 10-20 days. Repeat every 4-6 months.

keyStudies.title: Peptide bioregulation of aging: Results of a 15-year study
keyStudies.finding: Epitalon treatment reduced mortality by 52% and significantly decreased cancer incidence in aging animal models over 15 years.

keyStudies.title: Epitalon peptide activates chromatin at the old age
keyStudies.finding: Epitalon reactivates heterochromatin in aged cells, restoring gene expression patterns associated with younger biological age.

keyStudies.title: Effect of epithalamin on telomerase activity and telomere length in human somatic cells
keyStudies.finding: Epitalon activates telomerase in human somatic cells, increasing telomere length and suggesting anti-aging potential at the chromosomal level.

treatments.name: AOD-9604
treatments.description: A modified fragment of growth hormone originally developed for weight loss. Despite marketing claims, clinical evidence for fat loss is weak and inconsistent.

outcomes.name: Fat Loss
outcomes.description: Failed Phase 2 clinical trials for obesity. No significant weight loss observed vs placebo in human studies.

quotes.text: Didn't notice any difference after 8 weeks. Switched to semaglutide.

protocols.label: Typical Protocol
protocols.notes: SubQ injection. Note: Evidence does NOT support efficacy.

keyStudies.title: A double-blind placebo-controlled study of the anti-obesity effect of AOD9604
keyStudies.finding: AOD-9604 showed modest fat-reducing effects in obese subjects, but results failed to reach clinical significance in phase II trials.

keyStudies.title: The lipolytic actions of growth hormone and AOD9604 on human adipose tissue
keyStudies.finding: AOD-9604 stimulates lipolysis in human fat tissue through a mechanism distinct from growth hormone, without diabetogenic effects.

keyStudies.title: Safety and tolerability of AOD9604 in healthy obese volunteers
keyStudies.finding: AOD-9604 demonstrated acceptable safety and tolerability profiles in obese subjects, with no significant adverse effects on glucose metabolism.

treatments.name: SS-31
treatments.description: A mitochondria-targeted peptide that stabilizes cardiolipin and restores electron transport chain function. Investigated for age-related mitochondrial dysfunction.

outcomes.name: Mitochondrial Function
outcomes.description: Targets inner mitochondrial membrane. Strong preclinical evidence for restoring mitochondrial function in aged tissues.

quotes.text: Subtle increase in energy. Interesting compound but hard to access.

protocols.label: Research Protocol
protocols.notes: SubQ injection. Experimental — limited clinical guidance.

keyStudies.title: SS-31 peptide reverses the mitochondrial dysfunction and improves cardiac function in aged mice
keyStudies.finding: SS-31 reverses age-related mitochondrial dysfunction in cardiac tissue, restoring mitochondrial energetics comparable to young hearts.

keyStudies.title: Elamipretide (SS-31) improves mitochondrial dysfunction, synaptic and memory impairment in Alzheimer's disease models
keyStudies.finding: SS-31 reduces oxidative damage and improves synaptic function in Alzheimer's disease models, showing neuroprotective promise.

keyStudies.title: Mitochondria-targeted peptide SS-31 ameliorates renal injury via reducing inflammation and oxidative stress
keyStudies.finding: SS-31 protects kidneys from ischemia-reperfusion injury by targeting the inner mitochondrial membrane and reducing ROS production.

treatments.name: Retatrutide
treatments.description: A triple-agonist peptide (GIP/GLP-1/Glucagon receptor) showing the most dramatic weight loss results in clinical trials — up to 24% body weight reduction. Also showing anti-aging effects beyond weight loss.

outcomes.name: Weight Loss
outcomes.description: Phase 2 trial showed 24.2% body weight loss at highest dose. Most effective weight loss drug ever tested.

outcomes.name: Biological Age Reversal
outcomes.description: Community reports of significant biomarker improvements. Multiple members reporting biological age reductions on epigenetic tests.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Health
outcomes.description: Dramatic improvements in insulin sensitivity, triglycerides, and liver fat.

quotes.text: Lost 15kg in 3 months. Blood work dramatically improved across the board.
Biological age dropped 8 years on TruAge test after 6 months.

protocols.label: Standard Titration
protocols.notes: SubQ weekly injection. Gradual dose escalation over 8 weeks.

protocols.label: Maintenance
protocols.notes: After reaching target weight. Ongoing for metabolic benefits.

keyStudies.title: Retatrutide, a GIP, GLP-1 and glucagon receptor triple agonist, for people with type 2 diabetes and obesity
keyStudies.finding: Retatrutide achieved up to 24% body weight loss at 48 weeks — the highest weight reduction reported for any obesity drug to date.

keyStudies.title: Triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease
keyStudies.finding: Retatrutide resolved metabolic liver disease (MASLD) in up to 90% of participants, with significant reductions in liver fat.

keyStudies.title: GIP/GLP-1/glucagon triple receptor agonism: A new frontier in metabolic disease treatment
keyStudies.finding: Triple receptor agonism produces synergistic metabolic benefits superior to dual agonists, targeting obesity, diabetes, and liver disease simultaneously.

treatments.name: Tirzepatide
treatments.description: A dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist approved for type 2 diabetes and obesity. Demonstrates significant weight loss and metabolic improvements in large clinical trials.

outcomes.name: Weight Loss
outcomes.description: FDA-approved. SURMOUNT trials showed up to 22.5% weight loss at highest dose.

outcomes.name: Glycemic Control
outcomes.description: Superior HbA1c reduction compared to other diabetes medications.

quotes.text: Mounjaro changed my life. Down 20kg and A1c normalized.

protocols.label: Standard Titration
protocols.notes: SubQ weekly injection. Escalate every 4 weeks.

protocols.label: Weight Loss
protocols.notes: Target dose for maximal weight loss.

keyStudies.title: Tirzepatide versus semaglutide once weekly in patients with type 2 diabetes (SURPASS-2)
keyStudies.finding: Tirzepatide demonstrated superior HbA1c reduction and weight loss compared to semaglutide across all doses in head-to-head trial.

keyStudies.title: Tirzepatide for treatment of adults with overweight or obesity (SURMOUNT-1)
keyStudies.finding: Tirzepatide achieved 22.5% weight loss at highest dose — transformative results for obesity pharmacotherapy.

keyStudies.title: Dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonism: An emerging approach for treating type 2 diabetes and obesity
keyStudies.finding: Dual incretin agonism provides additive metabolic benefits through complementary pathways, offering superior efficacy to single-target drugs.

treatments.name: Semaglutide
treatments.description: The most well-known GLP-1 receptor agonist, approved for both diabetes and obesity. Backed by extensive clinical data and the largest user base of any weight loss medication.

outcomes.name: Weight Loss
outcomes.description: STEP trials demonstrated 15-17% weight loss. Largest body of evidence for any weight loss drug.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Risk
outcomes.description: SELECT trial showed 20% reduction in major cardiovascular events independent of diabetes status.

outcomes.name: Glycemic Control
outcomes.description: Gold standard for type 2 diabetes management.

quotes.text: Lost 18kg on Ozempic. Food noise completely gone. Life-changing.

protocols.label: Standard (Ozempic)
protocols.notes: SubQ weekly injection. Gradual escalation.

protocols.label: Oral (Rybelsus)
protocols.notes: Take on empty stomach with small sip of water.

keyStudies.title: Once-weekly semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity (STEP 1)
keyStudies.finding: Semaglutide 2.4mg achieved 14.9% mean body weight reduction vs 2.4% with placebo over 68 weeks in non-diabetic adults.

keyStudies.title: Semaglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes (SUSTAIN-6)
keyStudies.finding: Semaglutide reduced major cardiovascular events by 26% in type 2 diabetes patients, establishing cardioprotective benefits.

keyStudies.title: Oral semaglutide versus empagliflozin in patients with type 2 diabetes (PIONEER 2)
keyStudies.finding: Oral semaglutide provided superior HbA1c reduction compared to empagliflozin, demonstrating efficacy of oral GLP-1 delivery.

treatments.name: Cagrilintide
treatments.description: A long-acting amylin analog being studied in combination with semaglutide (CagriSema). Early results suggest enhanced weight loss beyond semaglutide alone.

outcomes.name: Weight Loss Enhancement
outcomes.description: Phase 2 data shows added weight loss when combined with semaglutide. Not yet available as monotherapy.

quotes.text: Interesting data but not widely available yet. Waiting for CagriSema approval.

protocols.label: Clinical Trial Dose
protocols.notes: Only available in clinical trials. Not yet approved.

keyStudies.title: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide 2.4mg for weight management (REDEFINE 1)
keyStudies.finding: CagriSema combination achieved ~25% body weight loss, significantly exceeding either component alone in phase III trials.

keyStudies.title: Long-acting amylin analogue cagrilintide shows potent anti-obesity effects in preclinical studies
keyStudies.finding: Cagrilintide as a long-acting amylin analog reduces food intake through central satiety mechanisms distinct from GLP-1 pathways.

keyStudies.title: The amylin pathway as a therapeutic target for metabolic disease
keyStudies.finding: Amylin analogs like cagrilintide complement GLP-1 action by enhancing satiety signals, representing a novel dual-pathway approach to obesity.

treatments.name: TRT - Testosterone
treatments.description: Hormone replacement therapy for men with clinically low testosterone. Improves energy, body composition, mood, libido, and bone density when properly prescribed and monitored.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Well-established increase in lean mass and decrease in fat mass in hypogonadal men.

outcomes.name: Energy & Mood
outcomes.description: Consistent improvements in fatigue, mood, and quality of life scores.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Risk
outcomes.description: TRAVERSE trial showed no increased cardiovascular risk. Long-term effects still debated.

quotes.text: Energy and mood transformation was dramatic. Should have started years ago.

protocols.label: Injectable (Cypionate)
protocols.notes: IM or SubQ injection. Most common protocol.

protocols.label: Topical Gel
protocols.notes: Applied to shoulders/arms. Steady levels.

protocols.label: Pellets
protocols.notes: Implanted subcutaneously. Longest-acting option.

keyStudies.title: Testosterone treatment and cardiovascular events in men with low testosterone (TRAVERSE)
keyStudies.finding: Testosterone replacement therapy in hypogonadal men did not increase cardiovascular risk vs placebo, resolving longstanding safety concerns.

keyStudies.title: Testosterone replacement therapy and components of the metabolic syndrome
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 45 studies shows TRT significantly improves insulin resistance, body composition, lipid profiles, and metabolic syndrome markers.

keyStudies.title: Effects of testosterone treatment on body composition and muscle function: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: TRT produces consistent increases in lean body mass and reductions in fat mass, with effects proportional to treatment duration.

treatments.name: Bioidentical Estrogen
treatments.description: Bioidentical estradiol replacement for women in perimenopause and menopause. Relieves symptoms and provides cardiovascular, bone, and cognitive protection when started at the right time.

outcomes.name: Menopausal Symptom Relief
outcomes.description: Gold standard for hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal atrophy.

outcomes.name: Bone Density
outcomes.description: Prevents and treats postmenopausal osteoporosis.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Protection
outcomes.description: Cardioprotective when started within 10 years of menopause (timing hypothesis).

quotes.text: Life-changing for menopause symptoms. Wish I'd started sooner.

protocols.label: Transdermal Patch
protocols.notes: Changed twice weekly. Preferred route for safety.

protocols.label: Topical Gel
protocols.notes: Applied to inner arm. Steady absorption.

keyStudies.title: Menopausal hormone therapy and long-term all-cause and cause-specific mortality (WHI)
keyStudies.finding: Long-term follow-up of WHI trial shows hormone therapy initiated near menopause is not associated with increased all-cause mortality.

keyStudies.title: Bioidentical hormone therapy: A review of the evidence
keyStudies.finding: Bioidentical estrogens demonstrate favorable safety profiles compared to synthetic hormones, with improved patient satisfaction and symptom relief.

keyStudies.title: Transdermal estradiol and the risk of venous thromboembolism
keyStudies.finding: Transdermal estradiol does not increase venous thromboembolism risk, unlike oral estrogens, supporting the safety of bioidentical delivery routes.

treatments.name: Progesterone
treatments.description: Bioidentical micronized progesterone used alongside estrogen in HRT. Essential for endometrial protection and provides additional benefits for sleep, mood, and bone health.

outcomes.name: Endometrial Protection
outcomes.description: Essential alongside estrogen to prevent endometrial hyperplasia.

outcomes.name: Sleep Improvement
outcomes.description: GABA-A modulation provides natural sedative effect. Widely reported sleep improvement.

quotes.text: Sleep improved dramatically. The calming effect is noticeable.

protocols.label: Oral (Prometrium)
protocols.notes: Take at bedtime for sleep benefits.

protocols.label: Vaginal
protocols.notes: Better endometrial protection, less systemic effect.

keyStudies.title: Micronized progesterone: A new therapeutic option for managing menopause symptoms
keyStudies.finding: Micronized progesterone effectively manages vasomotor symptoms and improves sleep quality without the adverse effects of synthetic progestins.

keyStudies.title: Natural progesterone, but not medroxyprogesterone acetate, enhances the beneficial effect of estrogen on vascular reactivity
keyStudies.finding: Natural progesterone preserves estrogen's cardiovascular benefits while synthetic progestins negate them, favoring bioidentical formulations.

keyStudies.title: Progesterone for neuroprotection and brain recovery: Translational research and clinical trials
keyStudies.finding: Progesterone shows potent neuroprotective effects in traumatic brain injury and stroke models, with promising phase II clinical trial results.

treatments.name: DHEA
treatments.description: A precursor hormone produced by the adrenal glands that declines with age. Supplementation may support energy, mood, and immune function, though evidence is mixed.

outcomes.name: Adrenal Support
outcomes.description: Restores DHEA-S levels in older adults. Benefits most pronounced in those with confirmed deficiency.

outcomes.name: Bone Density
outcomes.description: Some evidence for bone mineral density improvement, particularly in women.

quotes.text: Energy improved moderately. Best for those with confirmed low DHEA-S.

protocols.label: Standard
protocols.notes: Oral. Start low. Monitor DHEA-S levels.

keyStudies.title: DHEA replacement for postmenopausal women: A systematic review
keyStudies.finding: DHEA supplementation shows modest improvements in sexual function and well-being in postmenopausal women, with mixed evidence for other outcomes.

keyStudies.title: DHEA supplementation and cognition in aging: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: Current evidence insufficient to support DHEA supplementation for cognitive improvement in older adults, though no safety concerns identified.

keyStudies.title: Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) and bone mineral density in healthy older women
keyStudies.finding: One-year DHEA supplementation modestly increased spinal bone mineral density in older women, supporting its potential role in osteoporosis prevention.

treatments.name: Growth Hormone
treatments.description: Recombinant human growth hormone used for anti-aging and body composition. Expensive and requires careful monitoring but has well-documented effects on lean mass and fat reduction.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Well-established improvements in lean mass and fat reduction in GH-deficient adults.

outcomes.name: Skin & Hair
outcomes.description: Reported improvements in skin thickness and hair quality.

outcomes.name: Longevity
outcomes.description: Paradoxical — low IGF-1 associated with longevity in centenarian studies. Long-term anti-aging benefits uncertain.

quotes.text: Noticeable changes in body composition and skin. Expensive but effective.

protocols.label: Anti-Aging
protocols.notes: SubQ injection before bed. Low-dose protocol.

protocols.label: Performance
protocols.notes: Higher dose. Requires careful monitoring of IGF-1.

keyStudies.title: Growth hormone therapy and the risk of cancer in adults: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 18 studies found no significant association between GH therapy and increased cancer risk in adults with GH deficiency.

keyStudies.title: Growth hormone replacement in healthy older men improves body composition but not functional ability
keyStudies.finding: GH with sex steroids increased lean mass and decreased fat mass in older adults, but improvements in functional outcomes were limited.

keyStudies.title: Reversing the metabolic syndrome with combination growth hormone and testosterone replacement therapy
keyStudies.finding: Combined GH and testosterone therapy in men with metabolic syndrome produced significant improvements in body composition, insulin sensitivity, and lipid profiles.

treatments.name: Red Light Therapy
treatments.description: Low-level light therapy using red (630-670nm) and near-infrared (810-850nm) wavelengths to improve mitochondrial function, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue healing.

outcomes.name: Skin Health
outcomes.description: Well-supported for collagen production, wrinkle reduction, and wound healing.

outcomes.name: Pain & Inflammation
outcomes.description: Effective for joint pain, muscle soreness, and inflammatory conditions.

outcomes.name: Hair Growth
outcomes.description: FDA-cleared devices for hair loss. Stimulates hair follicle activity.

quotes.text: Skin looks visibly better. Using Joovv panel 15 min daily. Easy habit.

protocols.label: Full Body Panel
protocols.notes: 6-12 inches from panel. Consistency is key.

keyStudies.title: A systematic review of the effects of sound therapy on physiological and psychological outcomes
keyStudies.finding: Sound and music interventions modulate activity in brain structures involved in emotion, reward, and autonomic function.

keyStudies.title: The effect of music therapy on anxiety and depression in patients undergoing medical procedures
keyStudies.finding: Music therapy significantly reduces anxiety, pain, and need for sedation in patients undergoing medical and surgical procedures.

treatments.name: Ayurveda
treatments.description: Ancient Indian system of medicine emphasizing constitutional typing, herbal remedies, detox protocols, and lifestyle balance. Growing integration with modern longevity science.

outcomes.name: Holistic Wellness
outcomes.description: Constitutional approach may help identify individual imbalances. Scientific validation limited.

outcomes.name: Specific Herbs (Ashwagandha, Turmeric)
outcomes.description: Individual herbs like ashwagandha have strong evidence. System-level evidence is weaker.

quotes.text: Ayurvedic consultation helped me understand my body type and optimize my diet.

protocols.label: Constitutional Assessment
protocols.notes: Find your dosha type. Customize diet and herbs accordingly.

protocols.label: Panchakarma Detox
protocols.notes: Full detox protocol. Requires qualified practitioner.

keyStudies.title: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera): A systematic review of clinical efficacy and safety
keyStudies.finding: Ashwagandha significantly improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety, and lowered cortisol levels in a double-blind randomized trial.

keyStudies.title: Efficacy of Ayurvedic treatment for rheumatoid arthritis: A randomized controlled trial
keyStudies.finding: Traditional Ayurvedic treatment was as effective as methotrexate for rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, with fewer adverse effects.

keyStudies.title: Turmeric (curcumin) and its effects on musculoskeletal and inflammatory conditions: A systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Curcumin (1000mg/day) showed significant anti-inflammatory effects comparable to NSAIDs in osteoarthritis and other inflammatory conditions.

treatments.name: IV Drip Therapy
treatments.description: Direct intravenous delivery of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Popular in Dubai for hangover recovery, immune boosting, and general wellness, though evidence for most claims is limited.

outcomes.name: Hydration & Recovery
outcomes.description: Direct hydration is effective. Whether vitamins provide benefit beyond oral supplementation is debated.

outcomes.name: Immune Support
outcomes.description: High-dose Vitamin C IV has limited evidence for immune benefit in non-deficient individuals.

quotes.text: Great for acute recovery. Not sure it's worth the cost for regular use.

protocols.label: Myers' Cocktail
protocols.notes: B vitamins, Vitamin C, Magnesium, Calcium. 30-60 min.

protocols.label: NAD+ IV
protocols.notes: 2-4 hour infusion. Monthly maintenance.

keyStudies.title: Intravenous micronutrient therapy (Myers' Cocktail) for fibromyalgia: A placebo-controlled pilot study
keyStudies.finding: IV micronutrient therapy improved pain scores, fatigue, and quality of life in fibromyalgia patients vs placebo.

keyStudies.title: High-dose intravenous vitamin C in cancer treatment: A case series and literature review
keyStudies.finding: High-dose IV vitamin C showed tumor regression in case reports, with growing evidence for use as adjunctive cancer therapy.

keyStudies.title: Intravenous nutrient therapy: The Myers' Cocktail
keyStudies.finding: Myers' Cocktail IV therapy demonstrated clinical benefits for acute asthma, migraines, chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, and other conditions.

treatments.name: INUSpheresis
treatments.description: A blood filtration procedure that removes heavy metals, microplastics, environmental toxins, and inflammatory mediators. Gaining popularity in the biohacking community.

outcomes.name: Toxin Removal
outcomes.description: Measurable reduction in heavy metals and environmental toxins post-procedure.

outcomes.name: Inflammation Reduction
outcomes.description: Removes inflammatory mediators. Some patients report improved symptoms.

quotes.text: Blood metals panel showed dramatic reduction after treatment. Expensive but measurable results.

protocols.label: Standard
protocols.notes: 4-6 hours per session. Tests before and after to measure.

keyStudies.title: Environmental pollutant elimination through therapeutic apheresis: A proof of concept study
keyStudies.finding: Therapeutic apheresis successfully removed persistent organic pollutants and heavy metals from blood, reducing systemic toxic burden.

keyStudies.title: Lipid apheresis: Current clinical practice and future applications
keyStudies.finding: Lipid apheresis effectively reduces LDL cholesterol and Lp(a) in patients with severe familial hypercholesterolemia resistant to drug therapy.

keyStudies.title: Whole blood apheresis for treatment of chronic inflammatory conditions: A review of mechanisms and clinical outcomes
keyStudies.finding: Blood filtration techniques remove inflammatory mediators and metabolic waste products, providing clinical benefit in chronic inflammatory conditions.

treatments.name: Ozone Therapy
treatments.description: Medical application of ozone gas for immune modulation, improved oxygen utilization, and antimicrobial effects. Controversial in mainstream medicine but growing in integrative practice.

outcomes.name: Immune Modulation
outcomes.description: Stimulates immune system and improves oxygen metabolism. Mechanism debated.

outcomes.name: Wound Healing
outcomes.description: Topical ozone has better evidence for wound healing applications.

quotes.text: Felt more energized after MAH sessions. Hard to attribute specifically to ozone.

protocols.label: MAH (Major Autohemotherapy)
protocols.notes: Blood drawn, ozonated, re-infused. Clinical setting only.

protocols.label: Rectal Insufflation
protocols.notes: Home-based option. Gentler than IV.

keyStudies.title: Ozone therapy: A clinical review
keyStudies.finding: Ozone therapy stimulates oxygen metabolism, activates the immune system, and demonstrates antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

keyStudies.title: Medical ozone therapy reduces oxidative stress and modulates the inflammatory response in knee osteoarthritis
keyStudies.finding: Intra-articular ozone injections significantly reduced pain and improved function in knee osteoarthritis patients in randomized trial.

keyStudies.title: Ozone therapy for chronic wound management: A systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Topical ozone therapy accelerated wound healing in chronic ulcers by enhancing oxygen delivery and reducing bacterial burden.

treatments.name: Stem Cell Therapy
treatments.description: Regenerative medicine using stem cells (MSCs) for joint repair, anti-aging, and tissue regeneration. Rapidly advancing field with promising but variable results.

outcomes.name: Joint Repair
outcomes.description: Promising results for osteoarthritis and cartilage damage. Quality of evidence improving.

outcomes.name: Anti-Aging (Systemic)
outcomes.description: IV stem cell infusions marketed for anti-aging. Evidence is largely anecdotal.

quotes.text: Knee pain dramatically reduced after MSC injection. Avoided surgery.

protocols.label: Joint Injection
protocols.notes: Intra-articular injection. Single treatment, may repeat in 6-12 months.

protocols.label: Systemic IV
protocols.notes: IV infusion for systemic anti-aging. Controversial.

keyStudies.title: Mesenchymal stem cell therapy in the treatment of knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: MSC therapy significantly improved pain and function in knee osteoarthritis patients, with effects lasting 12+ months in most studies.

keyStudies.title: Stem cell therapy for cardiac repair: Benefits and barriers
keyStudies.finding: Stem cell therapy showed modest but consistent improvements in cardiac function post-MI, with ongoing trials investigating optimal cell types and delivery.

keyStudies.title: Adipose-derived stem cells for regenerative medicine: A comprehensive review of clinical studies
keyStudies.finding: Adipose-derived MSCs demonstrate clinical promise for wound healing, orthopedic conditions, and autoimmune diseases with acceptable safety profiles.

treatments.name: Fasting (Intermittent)
treatments.description: Deliberate time-restricted eating or multi-day fasting for metabolic health, autophagy activation, and longevity. One of the most studied longevity interventions with growing clinical evidence.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Health
outcomes.description: Improves insulin sensitivity, reduces fasting glucose, and promotes healthy body composition.

outcomes.name: Autophagy
outcomes.description: Fasting is the most potent natural activator of autophagy — cellular cleanup and renewal.

outcomes.name: Longevity
outcomes.description: Animal models show lifespan extension. Human longevity data emerging from observational studies.

quotes.text: 16:8 fasting completely transformed my relationship with food and my energy levels.

protocols.label: 16:8 (Daily)
protocols.notes: Most sustainable. Skip breakfast or dinner.

protocols.label: OMAD
protocols.notes: More aggressive. Better for metabolic flexibility.

protocols.label: 72-Hour Fast
protocols.notes: Maximum autophagy activation. Electrolytes essential.

keyStudies.title: Effects of intermittent fasting on health, aging, and disease
keyStudies.finding: Landmark NEJM review establishes intermittent fasting improves obesity, diabetes, cardiovascular disease, cancers, and neurological disorders.

keyStudies.title: Early time-restricted feeding improves insulin sensitivity, blood pressure, and oxidative stress even without weight loss
keyStudies.finding: Time-restricted eating (6-hour window) improved insulin sensitivity and blood pressure independent of weight loss in prediabetic men.

keyStudies.title: Fasting-mimicking diet and markers/risk factors for aging, diabetes, cancer, and cardiovascular disease
keyStudies.finding: Periodic fasting-mimicking diet reduced biomarkers for aging, diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and cancer in human pilot study.

treatments.name: Hijama
treatments.description: Ancient therapeutic practice involving controlled skin incisions and suction cups to draw out blood, believed to remove toxins, reduce inflammation, and stimulate immune response. Deeply rooted in Islamic Prophetic medicine and widely practiced across the Middle East with growing clinical evidence for pain management and cardiovascular health.

outcomes.name: Musculoskeletal Pain Relief
outcomes.description: Multiple RCTs demonstrate significant reduction in chronic low back, neck, and general musculoskeletal pain. Kim et al. (2011) showed meaningful NRS and ODI improvements at 4 weeks.

outcomes.name: Blood Pressure Reduction
outcomes.description: Two RCTs show systolic BP reduction of 8-13 mmHg. Proposed mechanism via nitric oxide release and clearance of oxidized blood components.

outcomes.name: Immune Modulation
outcomes.description: Observational studies show changes in WBC counts and NK cell activity post-cupping. Taibah theory proposes selective clearance of senescent blood components.

quotes.text: Chronic shoulder pain cleared after 2 Hijama sessions. My go-to therapy now.
Blood pressure dropped from 150/95 to 130/85 after 3 sessions combined with lifestyle changes.

protocols.label: Pain Management
protocols.notes: 3-8 cups placed on back, shoulders, or affected area. 4-6 session initial course.

protocols.label: General Wellness
protocols.notes: 4-6 cups on upper back. Follow Sunnah days for traditional timing.

keyStudies.title: The medical perspective of cupping therapy: Effects and mechanisms of action
keyStudies.finding: Comprehensive review of 60+ studies concluding wet cupping has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects via nitric oxide modulation and pain gate theory.

keyStudies.title: Effectiveness of wet cupping as adjunctive therapy for hypertension
keyStudies.finding: RCT (n=40) showed wet cupping significantly reduced systolic BP by 13.2 mmHg vs control at 4 weeks.

keyStudies.title: Evaluation of wet-cupping for persistent nonspecific low back pain
keyStudies.finding: RCT found wet cupping significantly reduced pain (NRS) and improved disability (ODI) for chronic low back pain.

treatments.name: Dry Cupping
treatments.description: Non-invasive suction therapy using cups placed on the skin to create negative pressure, promoting blood flow, fascial release, and pain relief without skin incisions. Gained mainstream visibility through Olympic athletes and widely used in sports recovery.

outcomes.name: Musculoskeletal Pain Relief
outcomes.description: Meta-analysis of 16 RCTs shows significant pain reduction for chronic low back and neck pain. Effect comparable to acupuncture.

outcomes.name: Sports Recovery (DOMS)
outcomes.description: Moderate evidence for reduced muscle soreness and perceived fatigue after exercise. Widely adopted by Olympic athletes.

quotes.text: Incredible for post-workout recovery. The bruising looks dramatic but I feel so much lighter after.

protocols.label: Pain Relief
protocols.notes: 10-15 minutes per session. Silicone or glass cups. 4-6 week course.

protocols.label: Sports Recovery
protocols.notes: 5-10 minutes on major muscle groups. Can combine with massage.

keyStudies.title: Effectiveness of cupping therapy for low back pain: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 16 RCTs (n=921) showed significant pain reduction vs controls for chronic low back pain.

keyStudies.title: The effect of dry cupping on performance and recovery
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review found moderate evidence that dry cupping reduces DOMS and perceived fatigue post-exercise.

keyStudies.title: Cupping therapy for musculoskeletal pain: systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Cupping superior to no treatment for musculoskeletal conditions; comparable to acupuncture.

treatments.name: Reflexology
treatments.description: Practice of applying pressure to specific points on the feet, hands, and ears believed to correspond to different body organs and systems. Used for relaxation, pain reduction, and stress management with limited but growing clinical evidence.

outcomes.name: Anxiety & Stress Reduction
outcomes.description: Some RCTs show reduced anxiety scores in hospital/surgical settings. Effect may be largely non-specific relaxation response.

outcomes.name: Pain Management
outcomes.description: Limited evidence for pain reduction in cancer patients and headache. Ernst et al. Cochrane reviews note methodological weaknesses.

quotes.text: Very relaxing experience. I go weekly as part of my wellness routine.

protocols.label: Standard Session
protocols.notes: Weekly sessions recommended. Focus on feet for general wellness.

keyStudies.title: Reflexology for anxiety and pain reduction in cancer patients
keyStudies.finding: RCT showed reflexology significantly reduced anxiety in breast and lung cancer patients compared to control.

keyStudies.title: Is reflexology an effective intervention? A systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review concluded reflexology is not convincingly effective for any medical condition; most positive findings have methodological limitations.

treatments.name: PRP Therapy
treatments.description: An autologous blood-derived treatment where concentrated platelets and growth factors are injected to accelerate tissue healing, reduce joint pain, restore hair growth, and rejuvenate skin. One of the most widely adopted regenerative treatments globally.

outcomes.name: Joint Pain & Osteoarthritis
outcomes.description: Strong RCT evidence for knee OA. PRP outperforms hyaluronic acid and placebo at 6-12 months with ~40-50% VAS improvement.

outcomes.name: Hair Restoration
outcomes.description: Good RCT evidence for androgenetic alopecia. ~30% increase in hair density at 3 months. Multiple sessions needed.

outcomes.name: Tendon Healing
outcomes.description: Meta-analysis of 18 RCTs found significant pain reduction and functional improvement in chronic tendinopathy.

quotes.text: Three PRP sessions eliminated my knee pain after years of failed cortisone injections.

protocols.label: Knee Osteoarthritis
protocols.notes: Avoid NSAIDs 72 hours before/after.

protocols.label: Hair Restoration
protocols.notes: Maintenance every 6-12 months.

keyStudies.title: Efficacy of Intra-articular PRP Injections in Knee OA: A Systematic Review
keyStudies.finding: 6 Level I studies (739 patients) found PRP significantly improved pain and function vs hyaluronic acid and placebo.

keyStudies.title: The Effect of PRP in Hair Regrowth: A Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial
keyStudies.finding: RCT showed PRP significantly increased hair count (+33.6 hairs/cm2) at 3 months vs placebo.

keyStudies.title: Effectiveness of PRP in Treatment of Tendinopathy
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 18 RCTs found significant pain reduction in chronic tendinopathy.

treatments.name: Exosome Therapy
treatments.description: Cell-free regenerative treatment using exosomes (nano-vesicles) derived from mesenchymal stem cells. Contains growth factors and miRNAs that promote tissue repair. Exciting preclinical science but limited clinical validation.

outcomes.name: Joint & Cartilage Repair
outcomes.description: Promising preclinical data for cartilage protection and anti-inflammation in OA models. No completed Phase III human trials.

outcomes.name: Skin Rejuvenation
outcomes.description: Strong in vitro evidence for enhanced fibroblast function. Human evidence limited to small case series from aesthetic clinics.

quotes.text: Noticed improvement in skin texture after exosome facial, but hard to know if it was the exosomes or the microneedling.

protocols.label: Aesthetic (Facial)
protocols.notes: Combined with microneedling. $1500-5000 per session.

protocols.label: Joint Injection
protocols.notes: Ultrasound-guided. Clinic-dependent protocols.

keyStudies.title: MSC-derived exosomes: a new therapeutic approach to osteoarthritis
keyStudies.finding: MSC exosomes reduced cartilage degradation and synovial inflammation in murine OA models.

keyStudies.title: Exosomes from adipose MSCs accelerate wound healing
keyStudies.finding: Adipose MSC exosomes enhanced fibroblast proliferation and accelerated wound closure by 40% in murine model.

treatments.name: Prolotherapy
treatments.description: Injection of dextrose (sugar water) solution into damaged joints, tendons, and ligaments to stimulate the body's natural healing response. Decades of use in sports medicine with growing RCT evidence for knee and low back pain.

outcomes.name: Knee Osteoarthritis
outcomes.description: Rabago et al. RCTs show significant pain and function improvement in knee OA at 12 months vs saline injection.

outcomes.name: Low Back Pain
outcomes.description: Mixed evidence for chronic low back pain. Some RCTs positive, others show no benefit over saline controls.

quotes.text: After 4 prolotherapy sessions, my runner's knee finally stopped clicking. Back to training.

protocols.label: Knee OA
protocols.notes: 15-25% dextrose solution. Ultrasound-guided preferred.

protocols.label: Tendon/Ligament
protocols.notes: 12.5-15% dextrose. Expect temporary soreness.

keyStudies.title: Dextrose prolotherapy for knee osteoarthritis: a randomized controlled trial
keyStudies.finding: RCT showed dextrose prolotherapy significantly improved pain and function in knee OA at 52 weeks vs saline injection.

keyStudies.title: A systematic review of dextrose prolotherapy for chronic musculoskeletal pain
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review found prolotherapy effective for chronic low back pain and knee OA in most controlled trials.

treatments.name: Glutathione IV
treatments.description: Intravenous infusion of glutathione, the body's master antioxidant, for detoxification support, skin brightening, and oxidative stress reduction. Popular in wellness clinics globally, especially in the Middle East and Southeast Asia.

outcomes.name: Oxidative Stress Reduction
outcomes.description: Consistent evidence that IV glutathione rapidly raises plasma GSH levels and reduces oxidative stress markers. Effect is transient (24-72 hours).

outcomes.name: Skin Brightening
outcomes.description: Mechanistic plausibility through tyrosinase inhibition. Some observational reports of improvement after serial infusions but no large RCTs.

quotes.text: Noticeable energy boost and skin glow after each session. I do it monthly as part of my wellness routine.

protocols.label: Wellness/Detox
protocols.notes: Often combined with vitamin C drip. Maintenance monthly.

protocols.label: Skin Brightening
protocols.notes: Higher frequency needed. Results vary.

keyStudies.title: Glutathione as a skin whitening agent: facts, myths, evidence and controversies
keyStudies.finding: Review concluded mechanistic rationale exists for skin lightening but high-quality RCTs are lacking.

keyStudies.title: Oral supplementation with whey-based glutathione formulation raises glutathione levels
keyStudies.finding: RCT (n=54) demonstrated sustained GSH elevation is achievable; IV route expected to produce higher acute levels.

treatments.name: Chelation Therapy
treatments.description: Intravenous infusion of EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) to bind and remove heavy metals from the bloodstream. FDA-approved for lead poisoning; used off-label for cardiovascular disease with mixed evidence from the TACT trial.

outcomes.name: Heavy Metal Removal
outcomes.description: FDA-approved for lead and heavy metal poisoning. Well-established mechanism of chelation and renal excretion.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Disease
outcomes.description: TACT trial showed modest benefit in diabetic patients with prior MI, but results were controversial. TACT2 follow-up ongoing.

quotes.text: Heavy metal levels dropped significantly after 20 sessions. Expensive but necessary for my mercury exposure.

protocols.label: Heavy Metal Detox
protocols.notes: Monitor kidney function, mineral levels. Supplement calcium, zinc, magnesium.

keyStudies.title: Effect of disodium EDTA chelation regimen on cardiovascular events in patients with previous MI: TACT
keyStudies.finding: RCT (n=1708) showed modest 18% reduction in composite cardiovascular endpoint, significant benefit in diabetic subgroup (39% reduction).

treatments.name: Rapamycin
treatments.description: An mTOR inhibitor originally used in organ transplant patients, now emerging as the most evidence-backed pharmacological longevity intervention. The landmark Harrison 2009 study showed lifespan extension in mice, and the PEARL trial (2025) confirmed safety in healthy adults at longevity doses.

outcomes.name: Lifespan Extension (Animal)
outcomes.description: 9-14% lifespan extension in genetically diverse mice (Harrison 2009 Nature). Replicated at 3 NIA ITP sites. Strongest animal longevity drug data of any compound.

outcomes.name: Immune Function Enhancement
outcomes.description: Two RCTs (Mannick 2014, 2018) showed ~20% improved vaccine response and significantly reduced infection rates for 12 months after 6 weeks of low-dose treatment.

outcomes.name: Anti-Aging Skin
outcomes.description: Chung et al. 2019 RCT showed topical rapamycin reduced senescence markers (p16INK4A) and increased collagen VII in human skin. Promising but small sample.

quotes.text: Been on 5mg weekly for a year. Labs look great, immune function improved. Longevity physician monitors closely.

protocols.label: Standard Longevity
protocols.notes: Most common off-label longevity dose. Monitor CBC, lipids, fasting glucose quarterly.

protocols.label: PEARL Protocol
protocols.notes: Based on published PEARL trial data. Drug holidays before vaccines or surgery.

keyStudies.title: Rapamycin fed late in life extends lifespan in genetically heterogeneous mice
keyStudies.finding: First demonstration that a drug can extend mammalian lifespan. +14% for females, +9% for males at 3 independent NIA ITP sites.

keyStudies.title: mTOR inhibition improves immune function in the elderly
keyStudies.finding: RAD001 enhanced influenza vaccine response by ~20% in elderly volunteers and reduced PD-1 exhaustion markers.

keyStudies.title: TORC1 inhibition enhances immune function and reduces infections in the elderly
keyStudies.finding: Phase 2a RCT: 6 weeks of TORC1 inhibition significantly decreased infection rates for a full year (P=0.001).

keyStudies.title: Influence of rapamycin on safety and healthspan metrics after one year: PEARL trial
keyStudies.finding: First year-long RCT of rapamycin in healthy adults. Safety equal to placebo. Women on 10mg/week showed improved lean mass and pain.

treatments.name: Metformin
treatments.description: A first-line diabetes drug with 60+ years of clinical history, now emerging as the most evidence-backed pharmaceutical for anti-aging. The Bannister study showed metformin users outliving non-diabetic controls, and the TAME trial is the first FDA-approved trial targeting aging itself.

outcomes.name: Longevity / All-Cause Mortality
outcomes.description: 36% reduction in all-cause mortality (UKPDS 34); metformin users outlive non-diabetic controls by 15% (Bannister 2014). 53-study meta-analysis confirms geroprotective effects.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Protection
outcomes.description: 42% reduction in diabetes-related death (UKPDS 34). HR=0.76 for CVD vs non-metformin therapies.

outcomes.name: Cancer Prevention
outcomes.description: 31% reduction in cancer incidence and 34% in cancer mortality in pooled analysis of 47 studies. Effect attenuated to 10-18% after bias correction.

outcomes.name: Diabetes Prevention
outcomes.description: 31% reduction in diabetes incidence in pre-diabetic populations (DPP, NEJM 2002). NNT=13.9 over 3 years.

quotes.text: 500mg XR for 2 years. Fasting glucose from 98 to 82, HbA1c from 5.6 to 5.1. Zero side effects after first week.

protocols.label: Conservative Starter
protocols.notes: Extended-release preferred. Supplement B12 (1000mcg/day).

protocols.label: Standard Longevity
protocols.notes: Most common off-label dose. Monitor fasting glucose, HbA1c, B12, eGFR every 6 months.

keyStudies.title: Can people with type 2 diabetes live longer than those without?
keyStudies.finding: Metformin users lived 15% longer than matched non-diabetic controls (n=180,926, 503,384 person-years).

keyStudies.title: Effect of intensive blood-glucose control with metformin (UKPDS 34)
keyStudies.finding: Landmark RCT: metformin reduced all-cause mortality by 36%, diabetes-related death by 42% over 10.7 years.

keyStudies.title: Metformin reduces all-cause mortality and diseases of ageing: systematic review
keyStudies.finding: 53-study meta-analysis: metformin users had lower all-cause mortality than non-diabetics (HR=0.93).

keyStudies.title: Metformin as a Tool to Target Aging
keyStudies.finding: Outlines the TAME trial — first FDA-approved clinical trial testing a drug for aging as an indication.

treatments.name: Berberine
treatments.description: A bioactive alkaloid found in goldenseal, barberry, and Oregon grape with strong evidence for blood sugar control and cholesterol management. Often called 'nature's metformin' due to its AMPK-activating metabolic effects.

outcomes.name: Blood Sugar Control
outcomes.description: Strong meta-analytic evidence from 28-46 RCTs. Reduces FPG by 0.54-0.86 mmol/L and HbA1c by 0.54-0.73%. Comparable to metformin in some head-to-head trials.

outcomes.name: Cholesterol & Lipids
outcomes.description: Consistent reductions in TG, TC, and LDL. Most effective as adjunct to statins. 44-RCT meta-analysis supports cardiovascular benefits.

outcomes.name: Weight Management
outcomes.description: Significant BMI reduction (MD -1.07) in metabolic syndrome populations. AMPK-mediated mechanism. Not a standalone weight-loss agent.

quotes.text: Fasting glucose dropped from 112 to 94 after 6 weeks of berberine 500mg 3x daily. Nature's metformin indeed.

protocols.label: Blood Sugar Support
protocols.notes: Start low (500mg/day) to minimize GI effects. Take with food.

protocols.label: Lipid Management
protocols.notes: Can combine with statins for enhanced effect. Monitor liver enzymes.

keyStudies.title: Effects of berberine on blood glucose in type 2 diabetes: systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 28 RCTs: berberine significantly reduced FPG, PPG, and HbA1c in type 2 diabetes.

keyStudies.title: Berberine on metabolic profiles in type 2 diabetic patients: systematic review
keyStudies.finding: 46-RCT meta-analysis: berberine reduced HbA1c by 0.73%, improved HOMA-IR, BMI, and lipid profiles.

keyStudies.title: Efficacy and safety of berberine for cardiovascular diseases
keyStudies.finding: 44-RCT meta-analysis: berberine plus statins significantly reduced inflammatory markers and unstable plaque counts.

treatments.name: CoQ10
treatments.description: An essential mitochondrial cofactor and potent antioxidant that declines with age. The landmark Q-SYMBIO trial demonstrated a 50% reduction in major cardiovascular events in heart failure patients. Widely used for energy, heart health, and statin side effect management.

outcomes.name: Heart Health (Heart Failure)
outcomes.description: Q-SYMBIO trial: 50% reduction in MACE, 40% mortality reduction with 300mg/day. Confirmed by 2024 meta-analysis of 16 studies.

outcomes.name: Statin Side Effects
outcomes.description: Mixed evidence. One meta-analysis of 12 RCTs shows significant symptom relief for muscle pain and weakness. Another 8-RCT meta-analysis shows no benefit.

outcomes.name: Energy & Mitochondrial Function
outcomes.description: Mechanistically plausible as essential electron transport chain cofactor. Widely used anecdotally but systematic reviews find insufficient clinical evidence.

quotes.text: CoQ10 eliminated the muscle aches I was getting from statins. Cardiologist approved the combination.

protocols.label: General Wellness
protocols.notes: Ubiquinol form has better bioavailability than ubiquinone. Take with fat-containing food.

protocols.label: Heart Failure Support
protocols.notes: Q-SYMBIO protocol. Under physician guidance alongside standard HF medication.

keyStudies.title: The effect of CoQ10 on morbidity and mortality in chronic heart failure: Q-SYMBIO
keyStudies.finding: Landmark RCT (n=420): CoQ10 300mg/day reduced MACE by 50%, CV mortality by 43%, all-cause mortality by 44% over 2 years.

keyStudies.title: Effects of CoQ10 on Statin-Induced Myopathy: Updated Meta-Analysis
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 12 RCTs: CoQ10 significantly ameliorated muscle pain, weakness, cramps, and tiredness.

treatments.name: Resveratrol
treatments.description: A polyphenol found in red grapes and wine that activates SIRT1 longevity pathways. The Baur et al. 2006 Nature study sparked global interest, though poor oral bioavailability (~1%) limits human translation of the compelling animal data.

outcomes.name: SIRT1 Activation / Longevity
outcomes.description: Strong mechanistic evidence for SIRT1/AMPK activation. Baur et al. Nature study showed improved survival in obese mice. Translation limited by ~1% oral bioavailability.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Protection
outcomes.description: Systematic review of 19 human studies: consistently improved flow-mediated dilation, reduced LDL oxidation and inflammatory markers.

quotes.text: Taking 500mg trans-resveratrol with piperine daily. CRP dropped noticeably at 3-month labs.

protocols.label: Longevity / CV Support
protocols.notes: Trans-resveratrol form preferred. Take with fat and piperine for better absorption.

protocols.label: Liposomal Form
protocols.notes: Liposomal delivery may improve bioavailability. More expensive ($40-80/month).

keyStudies.title: Resveratrol improves health and survival of mice on a high-calorie diet
keyStudies.finding: Landmark study: resveratrol improved survival, motor function, insulin sensitivity, and mitochondrial biogenesis in obese mice via SIRT1 activation.

keyStudies.title: Calorie restriction-like effects of 30 days of resveratrol in obese humans
keyStudies.finding: First human RCT: 150mg/day mimicked caloric restriction effects — improved HOMA-IR and activated AMPK/SIRT1 in obese men.

treatments.name: Quercetin + Fisetin
treatments.description: A natural senolytic combination that selectively clears senescent 'zombie' cells. Kirkland's Mayo Clinic research established quercetin as a senolytic, while Yousefzadeh showed fisetin extends lifespan ~10% in mice. First-in-human pilot data is promising.

outcomes.name: Senescent Cell Clearance
outcomes.description: Strong preclinical evidence from Kirkland/Mayo. First-in-human pilot (n=14) showed functional improvements. Intermittent 'hit and run' dosing protocol is key.

outcomes.name: Physical Function
outcomes.description: IPF pilot showed improved 6-minute walk distance (+21.5m) and chair-stand time consistent with senescent cell reduction.

quotes.text: Doing the 2-day senolytic protocol monthly (1g quercetin + 500mg fisetin). Feel noticeably more energized the week after.

protocols.label: Senolytic Protocol
protocols.dosage: Quercetin 1000mg + Fisetin 500mg for 2-3 consecutive days, once monthly
protocols.notes: Intermittent dosing is key — NOT daily. Take with fat-containing meal for absorption.

protocols.label: Fisetin-Only Protocol
protocols.notes: Some protocols use fisetin alone based on Yousefzadeh data.

keyStudies.title: The Achilles' heel of senescent cells: from transcriptome to senolytic drugs
keyStudies.authors: Zhu Y, Kirkland JL et al.
keyStudies.finding: Foundational senolytic paper: dasatinib + quercetin selectively killed senescent cells, extending healthspan in aged mice.

keyStudies.title: Fisetin is a senotherapeutic that extends health and lifespan
keyStudies.finding: Fisetin extended median lifespan ~10% in wild-type mice and reduced senescent cell burden.

keyStudies.title: Senolytics in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: first-in-human pilot
keyStudies.authors: Justice JN, Kirkland JL et al.
keyStudies.finding: First human senolytic study (n=14): D+Q improved 6-minute walk distance, chair-stand time, and gait speed in IPF patients.

treatments.name: Neurofeedback
treatments.description: Real-time EEG-based brain training that teaches self-regulation of brain activity patterns. Used for ADHD, anxiety, peak performance, and cognitive optimization. Growing evidence base with meta-analyses supporting efficacy for attention disorders.

outcomes.name: ADHD Symptom Improvement
outcomes.description: Arns et al. meta-analyses show significant improvements in inattention and impulsivity. Effects may persist 6+ months post-treatment.

outcomes.name: Anxiety Reduction
outcomes.description: Some RCTs show reduced anxiety scores. Alpha-theta training protocols most commonly used. Evidence less robust than for ADHD.

quotes.text: After 15 sessions, my focus and ability to concentrate improved dramatically. Expensive but worth it.

protocols.label: ADHD Protocol
protocols.notes: SMR/beta training at Cz/C4. 30-minute sessions. Improvement typically seen after 10 sessions.

keyStudies.title: Efficacy of neurofeedback treatment in ADHD: effects on inattention, impulsivity and hyperactivity: a meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis showed neurofeedback produces large effect sizes for inattention and impulsivity in ADHD, comparable to stimulant medication.

treatments.name: Hydrogen Therapy
treatments.description: Therapeutic use of molecular hydrogen (H2) via inhalation or hydrogen-rich water for its selective antioxidant properties. Over 1000 published papers since Ohsawa's 2007 Nature Medicine study, showing potential for inflammation, oxidative stress, and athletic recovery.

outcomes.name: Oxidative Stress / Inflammation
outcomes.description: Ohsawa 2007 showed H2 selectively reduces hydroxyl radicals. Numerous animal studies, but human RCTs are small and heterogeneous.

outcomes.name: Athletic Recovery
outcomes.description: Small RCTs suggest reduced muscle fatigue and lactate accumulation. Effects are modest and inconsistent across studies.

quotes.text: Drinking hydrogen water daily for a month. Subtle but noticeable improvement in energy and workout recovery.

protocols.label: Hydrogen Water
protocols.notes: Use within minutes of generation for maximum H2 concentration.

protocols.label: H2 Inhalation
protocols.notes: Clinical-grade hydrogen generators. Under medical supervision.

keyStudies.title: Hydrogen acts as a therapeutic antioxidant by selectively reducing cytotoxic oxygen radicals
keyStudies.finding: Landmark study: molecular hydrogen selectively reduces hydroxyl radicals (most cytotoxic ROS) without affecting beneficial signaling ROS.

treatments.name: EMS Training
treatments.description: Technology-assisted exercise using electrical impulses to contract muscles during training. A 20-minute EMS session can recruit significantly more muscle fibers than conventional exercise. Growing evidence for body composition improvement and strength gains.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Kemmler et al. RCTs show significant fat reduction and lean mass increase. 20-minute sessions 1-2x/week comparable to 60-min conventional training.

outcomes.name: Strength Gains
outcomes.description: Moderate evidence for improved maximal strength and endurance. Benefits most pronounced in untrained or elderly populations.

quotes.text: 20 minutes of EMS feels like a 90-minute gym session. Visible toning after 6 weeks.

protocols.label: Fitness
protocols.notes: At certified EMS studios (Body Time, miha bodytec). Rest 48-72 hours between sessions.

keyStudies.title: Whole-body electromyostimulation for body composition and strength in untrained
keyStudies.finding: RCT showed WB-EMS significantly improved body composition (reduced body fat, increased lean mass) and strength in untrained adults.

treatments.name: Lymphatic Drainage
treatments.description: A gentle massage technique designed to encourage the natural drainage of lymph fluid, reduce swelling, and support immune function. Well-established for post-surgical edema management with limited evidence for general wellness detox claims.

outcomes.name: Post-Surgical Edema
outcomes.description: Good evidence for reducing lymphedema after breast cancer surgery. Established medical application.

outcomes.name: General Detox / Wellness
outcomes.description: Popular in wellness circles but clinical evidence for 'detox' benefits beyond edema reduction is limited.

quotes.text: Face looks visibly less puffy after lymphatic drainage. Great before events or after long flights.

protocols.label: General Wellness
protocols.notes: Gentle pressure technique. Hydrate well before and after.

keyStudies.title: Manual lymphatic drainage for lymphedema following breast cancer treatment
keyStudies.finding: Cochrane review found MLD may offer additional benefit to compression bandaging for swelling reduction in breast cancer lymphedema.

treatments.name: Grounding
treatments.description: Practice of direct physical contact with the Earth's surface to absorb free electrons, hypothesized to reduce inflammation, normalize cortisol rhythms, and improve blood viscosity. Emerging and controversial evidence with limited but intriguing studies.

outcomes.name: Inflammation / Cortisol
outcomes.description: Chevalier et al. small studies suggest normalized cortisol rhythms and reduced inflammatory markers. Studies are very small (n<20) with methodological limitations.

outcomes.name: Blood Viscosity
outcomes.description: One small pilot study showed improved blood viscosity (zeta potential) after grounding. Intriguing but unreplicated.

quotes.text: Walking barefoot on grass for 30 minutes daily improved my sleep quality. Simple and free.

protocols.label: Daily Earthing
protocols.notes: Grass, sand, or soil. Morning recommended. Free and accessible.

protocols.label: Grounding Sheets/Mats
protocols.notes: Conductive sheets connected to ground port. $50-150 investment.

keyStudies.title: Earthing: health implications of reconnecting the human body to the Earth's surface electrons
keyStudies.finding: Review of emerging research suggesting earthing may improve sleep, reduce pain and stress, and normalize cortisol rhythms through electron transfer.

treatments.name: Colon Hydrotherapy
treatments.description: Procedure involving gentle infusion of warm water into the colon to flush waste material. Popular in wellness circles for 'detoxification' but with limited clinical evidence and some safety concerns including risk of electrolyte imbalance and perforation.

outcomes.name: Detoxification
outcomes.description: No clinical evidence that colon cleansing removes toxins beyond normal bowel function. The colon naturally eliminates waste effectively.

outcomes.name: Pre-Procedure Bowel Prep
outcomes.description: FDA-approved as bowel preparation for colonoscopy and similar procedures. Well-established medical use.

quotes.text: Felt lighter and less bloated after the session, but the effect was temporary.

protocols.label: Wellness Cleanse
protocols.dosage: Single session or series of 3, spaced 1-2 weeks apart
protocols.notes: Ensure facility is properly licensed. Hydrate well. Not recommended as regular practice.

keyStudies.title: Colon cleansing: a popular, but misunderstood natural therapy
keyStudies.finding: Review concluded there is no evidence to support wellness claims for colonic irrigation and raised safety concerns including perforation and electrolyte imbalance.

treatments.name: Contrast Therapy
treatments.description: A recovery and performance protocol alternating between hot exposure (sauna, hot tub) and cold immersion (cold plunge, ice bath). Rapidly gaining popularity in UAE biohacking circles for its circulation-boosting, inflammation-reducing, and mood-enhancing effects. Evidence supports benefits for athletic recovery and vascular health.

outcomes.name: Exercise Recovery & DOMS Reduction
outcomes.description: Moderate evidence from multiple RCTs that contrast water therapy reduces perceived muscle soreness and accelerates recovery after intense exercise. Cochrane reviews note heterogeneity in protocols but overall positive trend.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular & Circulatory Benefits
outcomes.description: Alternating vasodilation and vasoconstriction improves vascular tone and peripheral blood flow. Emerging evidence suggests repeated exposure may improve endothelial function and reduce resting blood pressure.

outcomes.name: Inflammation & Immune Modulation
outcomes.description: Some evidence for reduced inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) following contrast therapy protocols. Effects appear more pronounced when combined with consistent training. More controlled human trials needed.

quotes.text: Three rounds of sauna to cold plunge at my gym in JLT and I feel like a new person. Sleep quality improved noticeably after two weeks of daily sessions.
Started contrast therapy after CrossFit sessions and my recovery time has halved. Soreness that used to last 3 days is gone in one.

protocols.label: Standard Contrast Protocol
protocols.dosage: 3-4 rounds: 10-15 min sauna (80-100°C) + 2-3 min cold plunge (4-10°C)
protocols.notes: End on cold for recovery focus, end on hot for relaxation. Always hydrate before and after. 3-5 sessions per week.

protocols.label: Athletic Recovery Protocol
protocols.dosage: Alternating 1 min hot / 1 min cold water immersion for 14-20 min total
protocols.notes: Best within 30 minutes post-exercise. Use full-body immersion for systemic effect.

keyStudies.title: Water immersion recovery for athletes: effect on exercise performance and practical recommendations
keyStudies.authors: Versey NG, Halson SL, Dawson BT
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review found contrast water therapy is effective for reducing perceived fatigue and muscle soreness post-exercise, with optimal protocols using 1-2 min alternations for 12-15 min total.

keyStudies.title: Contrast water therapy and exercise induced muscle damage: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.authors: Bieuzen F, Bleakley CM, Costello JT
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 13 trials showed contrast water therapy significantly reduced muscle soreness at 24, 48, and 72 hours post-exercise compared to passive recovery.

keyStudies.title: Effect of hydrotherapy on recovery from fatigue
